category_title: Engaged elementary students with mild disabilities or general education needs
minimal_context: The student is participating in a small-group discussion about a cartoon panel showing eagles picking on a small bird to explore the feelings of bullying victims.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's look at this cartoon panel with the eagles picking on the small bird and discuss what is happening.

STUDENT: (Waves hands high) Oh! oh! They are bullying the small bird. I want to talk about this instead of making our own cartoons.

TUTOR: Okay, what do you think about the bullying in the cartoon?

STUDENT: It makes me really upset. I know about the recent deaths of students nationwide because of bullying.

TUTOR: What would you do if you saw someone being bullied like that?

STUDENT: (Facial expression changes to angry) If he saw somebody getting bullied, he would punch the one who started it!

options.A: Use the journalist method, speaking in short sentences like newspaper headlines to talk briefly and concisely about the cartoon's main details.
options.B: Start the lesson by defining bullying and asking, 'Who has ever been bullied?' and 'Who has ever been a bully?'
options.C: Shift the focus to a progressive and methodical tuning of awareness to each body part through a guided body scan.
options.D: Provide opportunities to respond (OTRs) during the discussion at a rate of four to six responses per minute with 80% accuracy.
